<a name="AllowMultipleStatementsInOneQuery"></a>
## func [AllowMultipleStatementsInOneQuery](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L224>)

```go
func AllowMultipleStatementsInOneQuery() func(*Loader) error
```

AllowMultipleStatementsInOneQuery is a performance tweak for running some setup statements in one query for better performance.

Your database and connection must be configured to support it: https://github.com/go-sql-driver/mysql?tab=readme-ov-file#multistatements

Only valid for MySQL as it is not enabled by default.

<a name="ClickhouseUseDeleteFrom"></a>
## func [ClickhouseUseDeleteFrom](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L429>)

```go
func ClickhouseUseDeleteFrom() func(*Loader) error
```

ClickhouseUseDeleteFrom If true, then "DELETE FROM …" syntax will be used to clean data from Clickhouse. Default syntax is "TRUNCATE TABLE …"

Only valid for Clickhouse. Returns an error otherwise.

<a name="DangerousSkipCleanupFixtureTables"></a>
## func [DangerousSkipCleanupFixtureTables](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L267>)

```go
func DangerousSkipCleanupFixtureTables() func(*Loader) error
```

DangerousSkipCleanupFixtureTables will make Loader not wipe data from fixture tables. This may lead to dirty data in the test database. Use with caution\!

<a name="DangerousSkipTestDatabaseCheck"></a>
## func [DangerousSkipTestDatabaseCheck](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L257>)

```go
func DangerousSkipTestDatabaseCheck() func(*Loader) error
```

DangerousSkipTestDatabaseCheck will make Loader not check if the database name contains "test". Use with caution\!

<a name="Database"></a>
## func [Database](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L107>)

```go
func Database(db *sql.DB) func(*Loader) error
```

Database sets an existing sql.DB instant to Loader.

<a name="Dialect"></a>
## func [Dialect](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L132>)

```go
func Dialect(dialect string, opts ...DialectOptions) func(*Loader) error
```

Dialect informs Loader about which database dialect you're using.

Possible options are "postgresql", "timescaledb", "mysql", "mariadb", "sqlite", "sqlserver", "clickhouse", "spanner".

<a name="Directory"></a>
## func [Directory](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L285>)

```go
func Directory(dir string) func(*Loader) error
```

Directory informs Loader to load YAML files from a given directory.

<a name="DumpDialect"></a>
## func [DumpDialect](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/dump.go#L51>)

```go
func DumpDialect(dialect string) func(*Dumper) error
```

DumpDialect informs Loader about which database dialect you're using.

Possible options are "postgresql", "timescaledb", "mysql", "mariadb", "sqlite" and "sqlserver".

```go
func DumpDirectory(dir string) func(*Dumper) error
```

<a name="DumpTables"></a>
## func [DumpTables](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/dump.go#L73>)

```go
func DumpTables(tables ...string) func(*Dumper) error
```

DumpTables allows you to choose which tables you want to dump.

If not informed, Dumper will dump all tables by default.

<a name="Location"></a>
## func [Location](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L342>)

```go
func Location(location *time.Location) func(*Loader) error
```

Location makes Loader use the given location by default when parsing dates. If not given, by default it uses the value of time.Local.

## func [Paths](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L313>)

```go
func Paths(paths ...string) func(*Loader) error
```

Paths inform Loader to load a given set of YAML files and directories.

<a name="ResetSequencesTo"></a>
## func [ResetSequencesTo](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L241>)

```go
func ResetSequencesTo(value int64) func(*Loader) error
```

ResetSequencesTo sets the value the sequences will be reset to.

Defaults to 10000.

Only valid for PostgreSQL and MySQL. Returns an error otherwise.

<a name="SkipResetSequences"></a>
## func [SkipResetSequences](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L205>)

```go
func SkipResetSequences() func(*Loader) error
```

SkipResetSequences prevents Loader from reseting sequences after loading fixtures.

Only valid for PostgreSQL and MySQL. Returns an error otherwise.

<a name="SkipTableChecksumComputation"></a>
## func [SkipTableChecksumComputation](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L277>)

```go
func SkipTableChecksumComputation() func(*Loader) error
```

SkipTableChecksumComputation will make Loader not compute table checksum at the end of the Loader.Load. This may improve performance. It may also slow down subsequent calls to Loader.Load, because checksums are used to not reload unchanged tables.

<a name="Template"></a>
## func [Template](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L356>)

```go
func Template() func(*Loader) error
```

Template makes loader process each YAML file as an template using the text/template package.

For more information on how templates work in Go please read: https://golang.org/pkg/text/template/

If not given the YAML files are parsed as is.

<a name="TemplateData"></a>
## func [TemplateData](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L413>)

```go
func TemplateData(data any) func(*Loader) error
```

TemplateData allows you to specify which data will be available when processing templates. Data is accesible by prefixing it with a "." like \{\{.MyKey\}\}.

<a name="TemplateDelims"></a>
## func [TemplateDelims](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L382>)

```go
func TemplateDelims(left, right string) func(*Loader) error
```

TemplateDelims allow choosing which delimiters will be used for templating. This defaults to "\{\{" and "\}\}".

For more information see https://golang.org/pkg/text/template/#Template.Delims

<a name="TemplateFuncs"></a>
## func [TemplateFuncs](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L367>)

```go
func TemplateFuncs(funcs template.FuncMap) func(*Loader) error
```

TemplateFuncs allow choosing which functions will be available when processing templates.

For more information see: https://golang.org/pkg/text/template/#Template.Funcs

<a name="TemplateOptions"></a>
## func [TemplateOptions](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L399>)

```go
func TemplateOptions(options ...string) func(*Loader) error
```

TemplateOptions allows you to specific which text/template options will be enabled when processing templates.

This defaults to "missingkey=zero". Check the available options here: https://golang.org/pkg/text/template/#Template.Option

<a name="UseAlterConstraint"></a>
## func [UseAlterConstraint](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/testfixtures.go#L174>)

```go
func UseAlterConstraint() func(*Loader) error
```

UseAlterConstraint If true, the contraint disabling will do using ALTER CONTRAINT sintax, only allowed in PG \>= 9.4. If false, the constraint disabling will use DISABLE TRIGGER ALL, which requires SUPERUSER privileges.

Only valid for PostgreSQL. Returns an error otherwise.

```go
func UseDropConstraint() func(*Loader) error
```

Dumper is resposible for dumping fixtures from the database into a directory.

```go
type Dumper struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewDumper"></a>
### func [NewDumper](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/dump.go#L27>)

```go
func NewDumper(options ...func(*Dumper) error) (*Dumper, error)
```

NewDumper creates a new dumper with the given options.

The "DumpDatabase", "DumpDialect" and "DumpDirectory" options are required.

# shared

```go
import "github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/v3/shared"
```

Shared code between implementation and tests. DO NOT IMPORT IT as the contract may change between versions

## Constants

<a name="ErrorMessage_NotSupportedLoadingMethod"></a>

```go
const ErrorMessage_NotSupportedLoadingMethod = `
testfixtures: %s is not supported for Spanner to ensure support for INTERLEAVED tables. 
Use Files():
  ensure the order of the files is correct, parents loaded before children or 
Use FilesMultiTables():
  and order your table keys in the yaml files from parent to child`
```

<a name="SpannerConstraintsQuery"></a>

```go
const SpannerConstraintsQuery = `
	SELECT 
			tc.TABLE_NAME AS table_name,
			tc.CONSTRAINT_NAME AS constraint_name,
			kcu.COLUMN_NAME AS column_name,
			kcu.ORDINAL_POSITION AS position,
			kcu2.TABLE_NAME AS referenced_table,
			kcu2.COLUMN_NAME AS referenced_column
		FROM information_schema.TABLE_CONSTRAINTS tc
		JOIN information_schema.KEY_COLUMN_USAGE kcu
			ON tc.CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA = kcu.CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA
			AND tc.CONSTRAINT_NAME = kcu.CONSTRAINT_NAME
		JOIN information_schema.REFERENTIAL_CONSTRAINTS rc
			ON tc.CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA = rc.CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA
			AND tc.CONSTRAINT_NAME = rc.CONSTRAINT_NAME
		JOIN information_schema.KEY_COLUMN_USAGE kcu2
			ON rc.UNIQUE_CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA = kcu2.CONSTRAINT_SCHEMA
			AND rc.UNIQUE_CONSTRAINT_NAME = kcu2.CONSTRAINT_NAME
			AND kcu.ORDINAL_POSITION = kcu2.ORDINAL_POSITION
		WHERE tc.CONSTRAINT_TYPE = 'FOREIGN KEY'
		ORDER BY tc.TABLE_NAME, tc.CONSTRAINT_NAME, kcu.ORDINAL_POSITION;
`
```

<a name="Queryable"></a>
## type [Queryable](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/shared/shared.go#L8-L12>)



```go
type Queryable interface {
    Exec(string, ...any) (sql.Result, error)
    Query(string, ...any) (*sql.Rows, error)
    QueryRow(string, ...any) *sql.Row
}
```

<a name="SpannerConstraint"></a>
## type [SpannerConstraint](<https://github.com/go-testfixtures/testfixtures/blob/master/shared/shared.go#L14-L21>)



```go
type SpannerConstraint struct {
    TableName        string
    ConstraintName   string
    ColumnName       string
    Position         int
    ReferencedTable  string
    ReferencedColumn string
}
```
